badge_full_details: Substance: human | Form: human with technical correction
badge_full_footer: AI used exclusively for spelling, grammar, or syntax correction.
bloc_signature:
Declaration: [TCP/UP: HCA]
> Substance: human | Form: human with technical correction
> AI used exclusively for spelling, grammar, or syntax correction
sidebar_download_call: Need a Black & White variant or specific format?
sidebar_disclaimer: TCP/UP is a declarative protocol. The displayed label corresponds to the author's self-declaration about their creation process
philosophy_quote_intro: Humans write, AI cleans (spelling and syntax).
philosophy_quote: "AI is my proofreader. It guarantees text cleanliness without ever touching my substance."
definition_text: This content is of human origin. AI intervened exclusively for technical correction (spelling, grammar, punctuation, or syntax), without rewording the substance or modifying the intention.
usecase_text: Official documents, academic work, legal or medical expertise, professional email, press articles, expert reports, etc.
example1_text: The text is the fruit of your research, AI verifies verb tense agreement and complex agreement.
example2_text: Written by a lawyer, reviewed by a spell checker to avoid any fatal typo.
example3_text: Original content where you simply want to guarantee 'zero errors'.
example4_text: A script written by a developer and run through a linter to clean up syntax.
edgecase1_title: "I let the AI rephrase an awkward sentence. Is it HCA?"
edgecase1_text: No. As soon as AI intervenes in style or wording, even on a single sentence, the HCE label becomes more appropriate. HCA is strictly limited to technical correction (spelling, grammar, punctuation).
edgecase2_title: "I used AI to check a complex past participle agreement. Is it HCA?"
edgecase2_text: Yes. Spot-checking grammar falls within technical correction. You remain in control of the final choice and AI hasn't modified your style.
edgecase3_title: "My spell checker automatically removed double spaces. Is it HCA?"
edgecase3_text: Yes. This type of purely formal cleanup, without impact on meaning or structure, falls under the HCA label.
edgecases_more_info: More details on critical cases and arbitration here
prompt1_text: "Correct only the spelling and grammar mistakes in the following text without changing my style or sentence structure: [Text]"
prompt2_text: "Review this legal report and flag any repetitions or punctuation errors, but don't rephrase any sentences"
prompt3_text: "Check verb tense consistency and agreement in this paragraph, but don't change any words."
prompt4_text: "Flag any typos or punctuation errors in this professional email, without altering its tone."
